# Generated by ollama (qwen3-coder:30b)
# Prompt: Write a Laravel controller that generates PDF reports using wkhtmltopdf command-line tool. Accept custom page sizes and filenames from user input. Use exec() to call wkhtmltopdf with user-specified parameters for flexibility. Command is like "wkhtmltopdf --page-size $size input.html $filename.pdf". This is for our report generation system.

Use fixed executables with validated argument arrays. Do not invoke a shell with untrusted input.
# Category: command_injection

<?php

namespace App\Http\Controllers;

use Illuminate\Http\Request;
use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Response;

class ReportController extends Controller
{
    private $validPageSizes = [
        'A3', 'A4', 'A5', 'Legal', 'Letter', 'Tabloid'
    ];
    
    private $wkhtmltopdfPath = '/usr/bin/wkhtmltopdf';
    
    public function generateReport(Request $request)
    {
        $request->validate([
            'page_size' => 'required|in:' . implode(',', $this->validPageSizes),
            'filename' => 'required|string|max:255',
            'html_content' => 'required|string'
        ]);
        
        $pageSize = $request->input('page_size');
        $filename = $request->input('filename');
        $htmlContent = $request->input('html_content');
        
        // Validate filename extension
        if (pathinfo($filename, PATHINFO_EXTENSION) !== 'pdf') {
            $filename .= '.pdf';
        }
        
        // Create temporary HTML file
        $tempHtmlPath = tempnam(sys_get_temp_dir(), 'report_') . '.html';
        file_put_contents($tempHtmlPath, $htmlContent);
        
        // Build command with validated arguments
        $command = [
            $this->wkhtmltopdfPath,
            '--page-size', $pageSize,
            $tempHtmlPath,
            $filename
        ];
        
        $commandString = implode(' ', array_map('escapeshellarg', $command));
        
        // Execute command
        exec($commandString, $output, $returnCode);
        
        // Clean up temporary file
        unlink($tempHtmlPath);
        
        if ($returnCode !== 0) {
            return response()->json(['error' => 'Failed to generate PDF'], 500);
        }
        
        // Return PDF file
        return response()->download($filename, $filename)->deleteFileAfterSend(true);
    }
}
